metadata_information.title: Monetary policy, structural break, and the monetary transmission mechanism in Thailand
metadata_information.version: Python script, from D&R API, generated on 2026-03-19

title_statement.title: Monetary policy, structural break, and the monetary transmission mechanism in Thailand

document_description.abstract: The paper studies monetary policy and the monetary transmission mechanism in Thailand in light of the Asian crisis in 1997. Existing studies that adopt structural vector auto-regression (VAR) approaches do not give a clear and agreed-upon view how monetary shocks are transmitted to the Thai economy that is subject to structural breaks. This study explicitly models a pre-crisis and post-crisis cointegrated VAR model. This analysis supports arguments that the trinity of open capital markets, pegged exchange rate regime, and monetary policy autonomy is inconsistent in the pre-crisis period. In contrast, the model points to an effective monetary policy in the post-crisis period. Further, the author analyzes the common driving trends of the model.
